title: El Senador Dave Cortese presentó hoy una legislación que ayudará a las comunidades a prepararse para el cierre repentino de salas psiquiátricas y de maternidad en los hospitales. E

{
  "claims": [
    "California has experienced a significant number of maternity ward closures, with at least 46 units closed in the last twelve years.",
    "More than half of the maternity ward closures in California occurred in the last three years.",
    "The closure of maternity wards disproportionately affects lower-income African American and Latina communities in California.",
    "The state of California faces a severe shortage of mental health beds.",
    "The success of the Community Assistance, Recovery, and Empowerment (CARE) program depends on the availability of mental health facilities.",
    "The closure of psychiatric units can be catastrophic for families and create a public health crisis.",
    "Unexpected closures of hospitals in low-income areas contribute to a demarcation known as the 'red line' of underrepresented populations deprived of essential services.",
    "The SB 1300 bill will require hospitals to provide 120 days' notice before closing maternity or psychiatric units.",
    "SB 1300 mandates hospitals to submit a comprehensive report detailing the potential impact of unit closures on the community.",
    "The sudden closure of psychiatric centers leads to longer wait times, negatively affecting individuals in crisis."
